description: Whether hooks can filter which tools or events they respond to using name matching, regex patterns, or structured criteria.

amp.mechanism: Amp hooks filter firing via 'input.contains' — exact literal substring match against tool input

claude-code.mechanism: hook_matcher_patterns: exact string, pipe-separated list, or JavaScript regex; matches on tool_name or event-specific fields; if field uses permission-rule syntax for per-subcommand filtering

cline.mechanism: Cline hooks fire on configured lifecycle events; no per-tool pattern matching documented

codex.mechanism: hook_matcher: Codex hooks support pattern matching to filter which tools or events trigger the hook

copilot-cli.mechanism: Copilot CLI hooks fire on all events of their configured type; no per-tool filtering documented

crush.mechanism: HookConfig has a matcher field: a regex pattern tested against the tool name. An empty matcher matches all tools.

cursor.mechanism: Hook entries accept a matcher string that filters which tool or event fires the hook (for example a specific shell command or tool name).

factory-droid.mechanism: PreToolUse and PostToolUse hooks use matcher strings supporting exact tool names, regex patterns (Edit|Create, Notebook.*), MCP tool patterns (mcp__<server>__<tool>), and wildcard (*) to match all tools

gemini-cli.mechanism: hook_matchers: Gemini CLI hooks support event and tool name matching to filter when hooks fire

kiro.mechanism: Kiro hooks fire on configured lifecycle events; no per-tool name filtering or regex matching documented

pi.mechanism: Pi hooks fire on configured lifecycle events; no per-tool name filtering at the subscription level — tool discrimination is done in handler code

windsurf.mechanism: Windsurf hooks fire on all events of their configured type; no per-tool or regex matching documented
